texto: Going home means going back in time. It's not a trip you care to take alone, and anyway, isn't that the main reason to take a mate? So you have an ally in the civil war against your parents?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Diana Joseph que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Going home means going back in time. It's not a trip you care to take alone, and anyway, isn't th…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, parents, relationships, com foco em relationships.

frase_original: Going home means going back in time. It's not a trip you care to take alone, and anyway, isn't that the main reason to take a mate? So you have an ally in the civil war against your parents?

texto: ...maybe she's a slut because she's lonely, she's sad, she's hoping someone or something will make the lonely and sad go away.It won't, of course. It never does. But nonetheless, there's not a girl who's more hopeful than a slut, more optimistic. She may give in but she doesn't give up. She keeps looking, she keeps hoping, she's always waiting for that someone who will say it: I love you, too.
explicacao: Citação de Diana Joseph sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “...maybe she's a slut because she's lonely, she's sad, she's hoping someone or something will mak…” articula amor, esperanca, loneliness em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como loneliness e love.

frase_original: ...maybe she's a slut because she's lonely, she's sad, she's hoping someone or something will make the lonely and sad go away.It won't, of course. It never does. But nonetheless, there's not a girl who's more hopeful than a slut, more optimistic. She may give in but she doesn't give up. She keeps looking, she keeps hoping, she's always waiting for that someone who will say it: I love you, too.
